problem: Why does acute pain become chronic in some people — what neural change occurs?
hypothesis: The key to Why does acute pain become chronic in some people — what neural change occurs? is Monoclonal antibody blocking protein-protein interaction — specifically Monoclonal antibody blocking — which would help 50,000,000 people affected.

experiment.protocol: Randomized controlled trial Phase I with 5 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: The impediment to action advances action. What stands in the way becomes the way. — Marcus Aurelius
